question: what is 369
content:
The 3-6-9 Spare Shooting System is a straightforward and widely used method for bowlers to consistently convert spares. Its core principle is to simplify spare shooting by maintaining a single target, Arrow 4, for every spare. Instead of altering the target or ball motion, all adjustments are made by changing the bowler's feet position on the approach. The system emphasizes throwing the ball straight, without any hook, to ensure predictability.

A common mistake bowlers make is trying to adjust their target or ball path for every spare, which can lead to inconsistency. The 3-6-9 system eliminates this by fixing the target and focusing solely on foot adjustments. Another frequent error is not having a consistent reference point. The system addresses this by establishing a 'Zero Position,' which is the spot on the approach from where a straight ball aimed at Arrow 4 will hit the center of Pin 1. This Zero Position becomes the unchangeable base for all subsequent spare adjustments. The golden rule for alignment is simple: for RIGHT side spares, move your feet LEFT (toward the LEFT SIDE of the lane), and for LEFT side spares, move your feet RIGHT (toward the RIGHT SIDE of the lane). You want to attack the pins in diagonal passing the ball though the middle arrow.

Mini-drill: To find your Zero Position, stand on the approach, aim straight at Arrow 4, and throw the ball with no hook. Adjust your feet until the ball consistently hits the center of Pin 1. Mentally mark this spot as your Zero Position.
